question: Explain the flexibility of the NuScale site layout and the number of modules.

nuscale_ch01_0315.title: SRP 9.2.1, Rev 5: Station Service Water SystemII.1 Protection Against Natural Phenomena (GDC 2)
nuscale_ch01_0315.summary: The NuScale site cooling water system (SCWS) is not safety-related or risk-significant and does not provide essential cooling to safety-related structures, systems, and components (SSC). Regulatory criteria such as GDC 2, GDC 4, and GDC 5 are only applicable to ensure that failure of the nonsafety-related SCWS does not adversely affect safety-related SSCs, and the system's design and layout conform to these requirements. GDC 44 is not applicable to the SCWS.

nuscale_ch01_0051.title: Plant Features of Special Interest
nuscale_ch01_0051.summary: This section describes key plant features of special interest in the NuScale Power Plant, including the Central Utility Building (CUB) and Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs), which house essential but nonsafety-related equipment and systems. It also highlights human factors considerations, emphasizing fail-safe design, automation, digital controls, and workload management to minimize human error and meet regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0390.title: SRP 9.5.1.1, Rev 0: Fire Protection ProgramII.7 Operational Program and Proposed Implementation Milestones
nuscale_ch01_0390.summary: This section explains that the operational fire protection program and its implementation milestones are the responsibility of the Combined License (COL) applicant, not the NuScale design certification applicant. NuScale’s application addresses fire protection criteria only to the extent necessary to support COL applicants, noting that certain requirements, such as passive separation of redundant trains, may not always be feasible due to the modular design. Development and implementation of risk-informed, performance-based fire protection programs are also assigned to COL applicants.

nuscale_ch01_0031.title: Plant Cooling Water Systems
nuscale_ch01_0031.summary: The plant cooling water systems are essential for supporting plant operations and include several subsystems: the reactor component cooling water system (RCCWS), reactor pool and spent fuel pool cooling systems, and the circulating water system. The RCCWS and pool cooling systems are closed-loop and nonsafety-related, transferring heat from plant components and pools to the site cooling water system, while the circulating water system is open-loop and supplies cooling water to the turbine condensers, with excess heat removed via cooling towers.

nuscale_ch01_0391.title: SRP 9.5.1.2, Rev 0: Risk-Informed, Performance-Based Fire Protection ProgramAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0391.summary: The section explains that the development and implementation of a risk-informed, performance-based fire protection program (SRP 9.5.1.2, Rev 0) is not applicable to the NuScale design certification application. Instead, this responsibility lies with Combined License (COL) applicants who reference the NuScale design and choose to implement the relevant regulatory provisions.

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides a summary of the NuScale plant’s overall design, including key systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling water, waste management, and auxiliary systems. It explains that each COL applicant must reference the NuScale FSAR and provide site-specific details, with certain conceptual design information and security-related content clearly marked and outside the certified design’s scope. Figures referenced illustrate the division between certified and site-specific design elements and the general site layout.

nuscale_ch01_0034.title: Reactor Building
nuscale_ch01_0034.summary: The Reactor Building (RXB) is centrally located on the NuScale Power Plant site and houses the NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) along with essential systems for plant operation and shutdown. It is a large, reinforced concrete, Seismic Category I structure designed for safety against various hazards, and provides radiation protection for personnel; each NPM is placed in its own bay within a common reactor pool arranged in two rows.

nuscale_ch01_0108.title: 8.37 ALARA Levels for Effluents from Materials Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0108.summary: This section provides a legend for electrical symbols used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, specifically for single-line diagrams. It details the meanings of various symbols and notations for components such as transformers, circuit breakers, batteries, switches, and other electrical devices, including their ratings and configurations. This information ensures accurate interpretation of the facility’s electrical system diagrams.

nuscale_ch01_0116.title: Figure 1.7-3e: Piping and Instrumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0116.summary: This section provides a comprehensive list of abbreviations and symbols used as legends in Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs) for the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It categorizes identifiers for various analysis functions, measurement types, and instrumentation, enabling clear interpretation of technical drawings and system schematics.

nuscale_ch01_0033.title: General Arrangement of Major Structures and Equipment
nuscale_ch01_0033.summary: This section describes the general arrangement of major structures and equipment at the NuScale Power Plant, focusing on the reactor building (RXB). The RXB is a centrally located, reinforced concrete structure designed for safety and radiation protection, housing the NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) in a common reactor pool with individual bays arranged in two rows. The section also references detailed figures and chapters for further information on radioactive waste management and building layouts.

nuscale_ch01_0117.title: Figure 1.7-3f: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0117.summary: This section provides the legend for symbols used in NuScale's Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs), detailing the graphical representations for various instruments, sensors, and auxiliary devices. It includes symbols for sample probes, pressure and temperature sensors, flow measurement devices, level indicators, and analysis equipment, ensuring standardized interpretation of the diagrams throughout the safety analysis report.

nuscale_ch01_0032.title: Radioactive Waste Management System
nuscale_ch01_0032.summary: The section describes the NuScale Power Plant's radioactive waste management system, which handles liquid, gaseous, and solid wastes, with detailed discussions in Chapter 11 of the report. It also outlines the general arrangement of major structures, focusing on the Reactor Building (RXB), a reinforced concrete, Seismic Category I structure designed for safety and radiation protection, housing the NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) in a central reactor pool.

edges.description: Section A explains that the NuScale site cooling water system (SCWS) and related nonsafety-related systems are designed so their failure does not adversely affect safety-related structures, while Section B describes the Central Utility Building (CUB) as a nonsafety-related structure housing common utility services, illustrating how nonsafety-related systems are grouped and separated to support flexible site layouts and modularity.

Section A and Section B both discuss how NuScale's design conforms (or partially conforms) to specific regulatory criteria, illustrating the flexibility in applying regulatory requirements to different site layouts and numbers of modules by distinguishing between safety-related and operational program requirements.

Section B describes how multiple NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) can be incrementally added or removed from service to match site needs, while Section A clarifies that the site cooling water system is non-safety-related and its design ensures flexibility without impacting safety-related systems, thus both sections together illustrate how the plant layout and module number can be flexibly managed without compromising safety.

Section A and Section B both discuss how NuScale's design conforms (or partially conforms) to different regulatory criteria, with Section A focusing on the site cooling water system's safety classification and Section B on fire protection program applicability, both of which influence the flexibility in site layout and module number by defining regulatory boundaries and responsibilities.

Section A specifies regulatory conformance details for the site cooling water system, while Section B provides a general overview of plant systems including cooling water, thus Section B's general description is further detailed by Section A's specific regulatory discussion.

Section A discusses the regulatory conformance and safety classification of the site cooling water system, while Section B describes the general arrangement and layout of major plant structures, both providing information relevant to how the site layout can accommodate different numbers of modules and maintain safety functions.

Section A describes the Central Utility Building's flexible, nonsafety-related functions and spaces, which can be adapted to different site layouts and numbers of modules, while Section B references regulatory guidance (SRP 9.5.1.1) that NuScale partially conforms to, indicating that the plant's design—including layout flexibility—must address fire protection program requirements.

Section B explains that multiple NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) can be incrementally added or removed from service, which demonstrates the flexibility in the number of modules and supports the site layout adaptability described in Section A's general plant features.

Section A describes the Central Utility Building's flexible, nonsafety-related functions that can support various plant configurations, while Section B outlines regulatory fire protection criteria that the NuScale design (including flexible layouts and module numbers) must conform to, connecting the plant's physical flexibility to regulatory compliance.

Section A provides specific details about the Central Utility Building and its functions, which are part of the general plant description summarized in Section B, thereby specifying how certain plant features support the overall site layout and modular flexibility described in Section B.

Section A describes the Central Utility Building's functions and flexibility as a nonsafety-related structure supporting plant utilities, while Section B outlines the general arrangement of major plant structures, including the layout that accommodates varying numbers of modules, thus both sections provide complementary details about how plant layout and supporting structures enable flexible module configurations.

Section A describes the Central Utility Building's functions within the plant layout, while Section B provides electrical diagram symbols and transformer specifications, with both sections relating through the infrastructure and utility requirements that support flexible site layouts and modular configurations.

Section B describes the modularity and incremental deployment of NuScale modules, which demonstrates the flexibility of the site layout and number of modules, thereby providing evidence that supports the regulatory conformance and implementation milestones discussed in Section A.

Section A and Section B both address fire protection program requirements for NuScale's design certification, with Section A referencing general fire protection program implementation and Section B focusing on risk-informed, performance-based approaches, both partially conforming to regulatory guidance and thus structurally connected by their shared regulatory context.

Section A references regulatory guidance for fire protection programs applicable to design certification, while Section B provides the general plant description that would need to address such regulatory criteria, connecting them through the requirement that the plant layout and systems described in B must conform to the fire protection program requirements in A.

Section A outlines regulatory guidance and implementation responsibilities for fire protection programs relevant to design certification, while Section B describes the general arrangement and layout of the NuScale plant, which would need to accommodate such operational programs, thus Section A's requirements inform and constrain the layout flexibility described in Section B.

Section A describes the modular and flexible nature of the NuScale plant layout, allowing incremental addition or removal of modules, while Section B outlines regulatory fire protection program requirements that the design must conform to, indicating that the flexible layout described in A must still satisfy the fire protection criteria in B.

Section A provides specific operational details about the flexibility of bringing NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) online or offline individually, which elaborates on the general plant design and modularity summarized in Section B.

Section A describes the modularity and incremental deployment of NuScale modules, while Section B references the general arrangement and layout of the plant, indicating that the site layout accommodates flexible numbers of modules as described in Section A.

Section A describes the modular and incremental operation of NuScale modules, which allows flexibility in site layout and module number, while Section B provides electrical diagram symbols and transformer specifications that are necessary for configuring and scaling the plant's electrical systems to accommodate varying numbers of modules.

Section A references regulatory criteria and guidance that NuScale must consider in its design certification, while Section B provides the general plant description that demonstrates how the design (including site layout and number of modules) aligns with or implements those criteria.

Section A outlines regulatory guidance and acceptance criteria for fire protection programs that NuScale considers applicable to its design certification, while Section B describes the general arrangement and layout of major structures, including the number and placement of modules, which must conform to the fire protection requirements specified in Section A.

Section A discusses regulatory guidance for fire protection programs applicable to NuScale's design certification, while Section B provides detailed electrical system diagrams, with the connection being that the layout and number of modules (addressed in the analysis question) must conform to fire protection requirements as specified in Section A and be reflected in the detailed design documentation of Section B.

Section A provides a general overview of the plant design, including flexibility aspects, while Section B offers specific details about the layout and arrangement of major structures, directly illustrating how the number of modules can be accommodated within the site layout.

Section A provides a general overview of the plant layout and systems, while Section B offers detailed electrical system diagrams that specify components referenced in the general description, connecting the overall plant layout to specific module configurations.

Section A describes the general arrangement and layout of major structures in the NuScale plant, while Section B provides electrical diagram symbols and details that would be used to represent and plan the electrical infrastructure within that layout, connecting the physical site arrangement to its electrical design.

Section A provides diagram legends for instrumentation and analysis functions, which are referenced in Section B's description of plant features such as the Central Utility Building that houses utility systems requiring instrumentation, thus connecting the technical symbols to their application in plant layout.

Section A provides diagram legends for instrumentation and analysis functions, which are referenced in Section B's discussion of fire protection program implementation, as detailed instrumentation is necessary for demonstrating compliance with fire protection requirements.

Section A describes the general arrangement and layout flexibility of major plant structures, which directly impacts how systems like the station service water system (discussed in Section B) are integrated and protected according to regulatory criteria.

Section A describes the overall site layout and references Figure 1.2-2, which includes the Central Utility Building (CUB) detailed in Section B, showing how specific plant features fit within the general arrangement relevant to site flexibility and module number.

Section A describes the general arrangement and layout flexibility of the NuScale plant, which provides the physical context necessary for Section B's discussion of fire protection program implementation and regulatory conformance, as the layout directly affects fire protection strategies.

Section A provides detailed instrumentation and accessory device information that supports the flexible configuration of site systems, while Section B outlines regulatory criteria for the site cooling water system, connecting them through the design's ability to adapt module numbers and layouts without impacting safety-related systems.

Section A provides detailed legends for instrumentation and accessory devices used in plant diagrams, which are relevant to the utility systems and equipment described in Section B's Central Utility Building, indicating how specific components support the general plant features.

Section A provides detailed diagram legends for instrumentation and piping, which are part of the technical design documentation that supports the implementation of operational programs such as the fire protection program described in Section B.

Section A describes the general plant layout and systems, which inherently allows for flexibility in the number of modules, while Section B confirms that the site cooling water system's regulatory requirements are limited due to its nonsafety-related classification, supporting the flexibility in site layout and module count.

Section A describes the general arrangement and layout of the NuScale plant, referencing Figure 1.2-2, while Section B details specific features of the Central Utility Building, which is one of the structures included in the overall site layout described in Section A.

Section A describes the general arrangement and layout flexibility of the NuScale plant, which can influence how fire protection programs (Section B) are implemented and tailored to different module numbers and site configurations.

Section A provides diagram legends for instrumentation symbols, which are referenced in Section B's discussion of the general arrangement and layout of major structures and equipment, enabling interpretation of layout figures.

Section A and Section B both provide legends for piping and instrumentation diagrams, with Section A focusing on analysis function identifiers and Section B detailing auxiliary and accessory devices, together specifying the symbols and terminology used in the diagrams referenced elsewhere in the NuScale FSAR.

Section A provides diagram legends for instrumentation and analysis functions, which are referenced in Section B's discussion of the plant layout and systems, indicating that understanding the legends in A is necessary to interpret the layout and system diagrams mentioned in B.

Section A provides a general description of the plant layout, while Section B provides the diagram legends necessary to interpret detailed piping and instrumentation diagrams that would be used to understand or modify that layout.

Section A provides diagram legends for instrumentation and accessory devices that are used in plant systems, while Section B describes the general arrangement and layout of major plant structures and systems, indicating that the specific devices and connections detailed in Section A are components that can be flexibly arranged within the overall site layout described in Section B.
